Title: Successful Login
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Data:
- Valid email: user@example.com
- Valid password: P@ssw0rd
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email and password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result:
- User is redirected to the home page.
- No error messages are displayed.

Title: Failed Login with Invalid Email

- Invalid email: wronguser@example.com
- Valid password: P@ssw0rd
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the invalid email and valid password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result:
- User remains on the login page.
- An error message indicating incorrect email is displayed.

Title: Inserting a New Emergency
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data:
- Emergency name: Fire at Building A
- Description: Fire outbreak in Building A
- Region: Rome
- Province: Roma
- Date: 2023-10-15
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Inserisci emergenza" button.
2. Authenticate the user.
3. Insert the emergency details.
4. Confirm that all required fields are filled.
Expected Result:
- Emergency is successfully created.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Inserting an Emergency with Missing Details
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data:
- Emergency name: Fire at Building A
- Description: Fire outbreak in Building A
- Region: Rome
- Province: Roma
- Date: (empty)
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Inserisci emergenza" button.
2. Authenticate the user.
3. Insert the emergency details with missing date.
4. Attempt to confirm the emergency creation.
Expected Result:
- Emergency is not created.
- Error message indicating missing required field is displayed.

Title: Viewing Emergencies
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data: - Existing emergency: Fire at Building A
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Visualizza emergenze" button.
2. Check if the emergency list is populated.
Expected Result:
- List of emergencies is displayed.
- Emergency "Fire at Building A" is listed.

Title: Viewing Emergencies with No Records
Preconditions: No emergencies exist in the system.
Test Data: - No existing emergencies.
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Visualizza emergenze" button.
2. Check if the emergency list is empty.
Expected Result:
- Message "Nessuna emergenza" is displayed.
- No emergencies are listed.

Title: Terminating an Emergency
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data: - Existing emergency: Fire at Building A
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to /terminaEmergenza.
2. Verify the token.
3. Update the database to mark the emergency as terminated.
4. Retrieve the result.
Expected Result:
- Emergency is marked as terminated.
- Response message indicates one emergency terminated.

Title: Terminating Nonexistent Emergency
Preconditions: No emergencies exist in the system.
Test Data: - Nonexistent emergency: Fire at Building B
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to /terminaEmergenza.
2. Verify the token.
3. Attempt to update the database to mark the nonexistent emergency as terminated.
4. Retrieve the result.
Expected Result:
- No emergencies are terminated.
- Response message indicates no emergencies terminated.

Title: Assigning a Volunteer to a Task
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data:
- Existing emergency: Fire at Building A
- Existing task: Rescue operation
- Available volunteer: John Doe
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Visualizza emergenza" button.
2. Authenticate the user.
3. Select the emergency.
4. Select the task.
5. Assign the volunteer to the task.
Expected Result:
- Volunteer is assigned to the task.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Removing a Volunteer from a Task
Preconditions: Volunteer is already assigned to a task.
Test Data:
- Existing emergency: Fire at Building A
- Existing task: Rescue operation
- Assigned volunteer: John Doe
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Visualizza emergenza" button.
2. Authenticate the user.
3. Select the emergency.
4. Select the task.
5. Remove the volunteer from the task.
Expected Result:
- Volunteer is removed from the task.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Assigning a Volunteer to a Nonexistent Task
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data:
- Existing emergency: Fire at Building A
- Nonexistent task: Search and rescue
- Available volunteer: John Doe
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Visualizza emergenza" button.
2. Authenticate the user.
3. Select the emergency.
4. Attempt to select the nonexistent task.
5. Assign the volunteer to the task.
Expected Result:
- Volunteer is not assigned to the task.
- Error message indicating task does not exist is displayed.

Title: Token Generation and Verification
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data:
- Valid email: user@example.com
- Valid password: P@ssw0rd
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to /login.
2. Query the database for user data.
3. Verify the user length and password.
4. Generate a token JWT.
5. Update tokens and revoke the old one.
6. Send the response or error to the client.
Expected Result:
- Token is generated and verified successfully.
- Response message contains the token.

Title: Token Revocation
Preconditions: User is authenticated and has the necessary permissions.
Test Data:
- Valid email: user@example.com
- Valid password: P@ssw0rd
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to /login.
2. Query the database for user data.
3. Verify the user length and password.
4. Generate a token JWT.
5. Update tokens and revoke the old one.
6. Send the response or error to the client.
